---
title: "anomaly comparison graphs"
author: "Madhumita Kabade"
date: "2026-04-26"
output: html_document
---

# Description:
#This file is used to prepare and analyse environmental water-quality data
#for anomaly comparison graphs across the three study regions: Bowen, Mackay, and Weipa. The main aim is to build a clean merged dataset containing key physicochemical variables such as temperature, dissolved oxygen, pH, Secchi, salinity, total nitrogen (TN), total phosphorus (TP), and chlorophyll-a together with date, site, region, and seasonal information.

#The workflow in this file will focus on:
#1. cleaning and organising field and lab-based water-quality datasets
#2. creating time-related columns such as year, month, and wet/dry season
#3. summarising environmental variables at the chosen temporal and regional scale
#4. calculating anomalies relative to long-term or study-period means
#5. producing region-wise anomaly comparison graphs to examine how environmental conditions vary through time. 

#These outputs will be used to compare environmental patterns across regions and to support later interpretation of plankton richness, abundance, and community changes in relation to water quality.

# Reading the Physiochem Database File

```{r}
library(readxl)
library(dplyr)
library(lubridate)

#Read
excel_sheets("data/Physiochem Database tables_all_rows(20240902_1226).xlsx")

#Run
field_meas_raw <- read_excel(
  "data/Physiochem Database tables_all_rows(20240902_1226).xlsx",
  sheet = "Field_meas(20240902_1226)"
)

#Inspect
glimpse(field_meas_raw)
unique(field_meas_raw$Site_code)
```
